title: Xeriscaped Light Australian Oysters Poke
description: A refreshing, paleo-friendly take on traditional oyster poke, featuring Australian oysters, sea salt, and a light, zesty dressing.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together lime juice, ginger, garlic, and sesame seeds to make the dressing.
Add the dressing to the shucked oysters and toss to combine.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 10-15 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Just before serving, sprinkle the chopped parsley and mint over the top of the oysters.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional parsley and mint if desired.
Divide the oysters among individual plates or bowls and serve as a light and refreshing main course or appetizer.
This recipe makes 4-6 servings, depending on serving size.
Oysters can be shucked and stored in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before preparing the poke.
If serving as a snack or appetizer, consider pairing with toasted bread or crackers for added crunch.
